titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Charles Ives Oral History Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 27, 2019 at 00:00

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Ives Project was the first documentary oral history of an American composer. Interviews with sixty people who knew and worked with Charles Ives were conducted between 1968 and 1971, and this unit was the impetus for the founding of Oral History of American Music. Family, friends, neighbors, business associates, and musicians reminisce about one of the most significant artistic figures of the century. Some of the musicians are: Arthur Berger, Elliott Carter, Lehman Engel, Lou Harrison, Bernard Herrmann, John Kirkpatrick, Goddard Lieberson, Darius Milhaud, Jerome Moross, Dane Rudhyar, Carl Ruggles, Charles Seeger, and Nicolas Slonimsky. Charles Ives Remembered: An Oral History (Yale, 1974; Norton paperback, 1978) received the Kinkeldey Prize from the American Musicological Society in 1975. Excerpts from the taped interviews were used in a five record package, "Charles Ives 100th Anniversary" (1974) and in a film documentary, "A Good Dissonance Like a Man" (1977).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: OHII, Charles Ives Oral History, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Streaming access to sound recordings in this collection is available when connected to the Yale network, or otherwise by request.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for OHAM materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast OHAM materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.

odd.head: Recording missing
odd.p: No CD or digital file for this interview could be found in Feb 2019 when reviewing this interview's metadata.

odd.head: Interviewer
odd.p: SEE OH V #1 for all information
